title: Loops
summary: Repeat work while keeping the stopping rule visible.
refresher: A `for` loop places setup, condition, and update together. A `while` loop repeats while its condition stays truthy. Trace the initial state, boundary, and update.

blocks.body: A `for` loop places setup, condition, and update together. A `while` loop repeats while its condition stays truthy. Trace the initial state, boundary, and update. Repeat work while keeping the stopping rule visible.

A loop has four parts to reason about: starting state, condition, body, and update. Write those parts on separate lines when tracing. After each pass, ask what changed and whether that change moves the condition toward false. This method works for both `for` and `while`.

blocks.caption: The counter starts at zero, runs below three, and grows after each pass.
A strict upper bound keeps array indexes valid.

blocks.body: For three items, the corrected index values are zero, one, and two. After index two, the update makes `i` equal three, the condition fails, and the loop ends. The wrong version allows index three, which is one position past the array.

blocks.title: Check the boundary
blocks.body: When a loop hangs, locate the line that moves its tested state toward an exit; a missing update keeps the condition true.

blocks.why: The values one through four are included by the less-than-or-equal condition.
Count never changes, so the condition remains true and the loop cannot finish.
